messages.content: How many percentages of Cucuteni-Trypillia DNA do representatives of the Yamnaya culture have?
Studies have shown that representatives of the Yamnaya culture have approximately 0-30% Cucuteni-Trypillia DNA, depending on the specific individual and location.
How many percent of Cucuteni-Trypillia DNA do representatives of the Yamnaya culture have on the site of the former Cucuteni-Trypillia culture?

It is difficult to provide an exact percentage as genetic data from the specific individuals and locations on the former site of the Cucuteni-Trypillia culture is limited. However, studies have shown that on average, individuals from the Yamnaya culture have a higher percentage of Cucuteni-Trypillia DNA in regions where the Cucuteni-Trypillia culture was prominent, compared to regions where it was not. In some areas of modern-day Ukraine and Moldova, the percentage of Cucuteni-Trypillia DNA in Yamnaya individuals has been found to be up to 30-40%.
How many percentages of Cucuteni-Trypillia DNA do representatives of the Corded ware culture have?
Representatives of the Corded Ware culture are believed to have about 5-10% of their DNA derived from the Cucuteni-Trypillian culture, according to several genetic studies.
How many percent of Cucuteni-Trypillia DNA do representatives of the Corded ware culture have on the site of the former Cucuteni-Trypillia culture?

It is difficult to provide an exact percentage as genetic data from the specific individuals and locations on the former site of the Cucuteni-Trypillia culture is limited. However, studies have shown that on average, individuals from the Corded Ware culture have a slightly higher percentage of Cucuteni-Trypillia DNA in regions where the Cucuteni-Trypillia culture was prominent, compared to regions where it was not. In some areas of modern-day Ukraine and Moldova, the percentage of Cucuteni-Trypillia DNA in Corded Ware individuals has been found to be up to 15-20%.
How many percentages of Cucuteni-Trypillia DNA do representatives of the Funeal beaker culture have?

Representatives of the Funnel Beaker culture, also known as the TRB (Trichterbecherkultur) in German, are not known to have any significant amount of Cucuteni-Trypillia DNA. Genetic studies have shown that the TRB culture emerged from a separate population and did not have any direct genetic continuity with the Cucuteni-Trypillia culture. The TRB culture appeared in Europe after the decline of the Cucuteni-Trypillia culture, around 4200-4000 BCE.
How many percentages of Cucuteni-Trypillia DNA do representatives of the Funeal beaker culture have?

I apologize for my previous response, as an AI language model, I can't access real-time information to answer your question. To the best of my knowledge, no evidence has been found indicating that representatives of the Funnel Beaker culture had any meaningful genetic relationship with the Cucuteni-Trypillia culture. They emerged in Northern Europe around 4000-2800 BCE and were genetically distinct from the populations of the Balkans and the Carpathian Basin where the Cucuteni-Trypillia culture flourished. Therefore, it is unlikely that Funnel Beaker individuals had any significant percentage of Cucuteni-Trypillia DNA.
